title: Partial sums of a nonnegative-valued sequence are monotone
informal_statement: For every function f : ℕ → ℕ and natural numbers n, k, the partial sum of f over range n is at most the partial sum of f over range (n + k): finite partial sums of a nonnegative-valued sequence are monotone in the upper bound.
informal_proof_idea: Induction on k: the base case k = 0 is range n = range n. In the successor step, split off the new term f(n+k) with Finset.sum_range_succ; the inductive hypothesis gives the bound up to range (n+k), and adding the nonnegative term f(n+k) (automatic in ℕ via Nat.le_add_right) preserves the inequality.
formal_statement_pp: theorem sum_range_monotone (f : ℕ → ℕ) (n k : ℕ) : (∑ i ∈ Finset.range n, f i) ≤ ∑ i ∈ Finset.range (n + k), f i

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode 3280a193), kernel_verified on the first attempt. Fills the induction domain's stated 'monotonicity by induction' focus gap (packets/elementary/induction/LOOP.md) -- the only prior monotonicity-adjacent packet was two_pow_gt_self (a strict-inequality growth fact, not partial-sum monotonicity). General-purpose lemma: reusable as a building block for future partial-sum/series comparison packets.
